rationale: X's top comment (reuse pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded component list) echoes the spirit of dsocek's real concern about the hardcoded default_components list drifting and wanting a single source of truth, and X also explicitly notes the PR body's before/after images, addressing xuechendi's validation ask — giving it noticeably better recall. Y's output is mostly a validation checklist restating already-obvious facts, with only one weak finding (registry.py list divergence, fix suggested is just 'add a comment') that doesn't map to any ground-truth concern. Both sets of specific claims appear plausible and grounded, but X's two findings are more concrete and actionable (specific code-level fixes: read _dit_modules, add a warning log) versus Y's single low-value documentation nit.
